-- 1 of 25 --

Welcome to Manufacturing & Operations at Scale!
I’m Rich Li and I’m the co-founder of Brosa, an
online furniture retailer, and the luggage brand,
July. Within the first nine months of July, I helped it
secure $10.5 million in funding. Meanwhile Brosa has
become Australia’s largest online furniture retailer
worth over $60M AUD with over 3,000 different
products and 10,000 different SKUs.
Before BROSA and July, I built my experience in
ecommerce as the largest seller to Groupon Group
globally, sourcing and creating products from China
for retail sale in the US, Australia, Canada and New
Zealand.
In this module, I’ll teach you how I’ve set up the
entire supply chain operations for my businesses
from the ground up from product sourcing,
manufacturing, logistics all the way to the end
consumer.
LESSON:
MODULE INTRO

-- 3 of 25 --

In this lesson, I’m going to give you an overview of
the supply chain operations for ecommerce. Even
at this stage in your company, many founders still
consider this process complicated and difficult. I’ll
show you how every topic covered in this module fits
in the grand scheme of things. I hope to demystify
the process so you realize that specialized skills
aren’t necessary to run tight supply chain operations.
The Three Main Supply Chain Components
Sourcing
• 	Finding quality suppliers
• 	Setting up a good working relationship
• 	Negotiating and collaborating with suppliers
Production
• 	Placing an order
• 	Locking in a production slot
• 	Sourcing raw material
• 	Nailing down a delivery date
Logistics
• 	Freight forwarding (Supplier to warehouse)
• 	Warehousing (3PL or yourself)
• 	Last Mile Delivery (Warehouse to customer)
LESSON:
THE SUPPLY CHAIN PUZZLE
(PRIMER)

-- 6 of 25 --

In this lesson, I’ll teach you how to calculate your
Contribution Margin from an example P&L sheet.
This will help you understand how much you can
pay your supply chain partners. Ultimately, aim for
a Contribution Margin of 10%. That usually means
you’re set up to scale and put more money into your
supply chain, marketing or whatever else you think
needs a boost.
Contribution Margin
The sales amount left over to pay your fixed costs
(i.e., rent, utilities)
Contribution Margin = Gross Margin - Variable Costs
Variable Costs
Merchant facility costs
Credit card fees
Last Mile Delivery
Marketing costs
Raw materials
Contribution Margin helps you decide:
If variable costs for a product need to be REDUCED
If the price of the product needs to be INCREASED
If costs and product should REMAIN THE SAME
How to Calculate Contribution Margin
Step 1: Calculate Gross Profit
Gross Revenue - Discount = Net Revenue - Variable
Costs = Gross Profit
Step 2: Calculate Marketing Costs
Gross Profit - Marketing (CPC x Visitor Sessions) =
Contribution Margin
Step 3: Calculate Contribution Margin Percentage
Contribution Margin ÷ Gross Profit = Contribution
Margin (%)
LESSON:
THE FIRST STEP TO SCALING
(CONTRIBUTION MARGIN)

“To Scale or Not To Scale” Questionnaire
Use the questions below to determine whether your business is
ready to scale manufacturing and operations.
Circle YES or NO for each.
Is your product/brand proven? Do you have enough demand to be producing at large
quantities?
YES 	NO
Have you surpassed your revenue targets? Do you have repeatable sales?
YES 	NO
Are you in the market for global manufacturers?
YES 	NO
Is there minimal risk in scaling your business right now? Do you have enough cash
reserves to scale at low risk?
YES 	NO

-- 9 of 25 --

In this lesson, I’ll teach you my tried and tested
method for finding quality suppliers. As I mentioned
earlier, scaling manufacturing not only involves
making larger quantities of product but also
producing at a higher quality. And at the end of the
day quality products mean more positive feedback
and a higher Customer Lifetime Value (CLV).
The Best Suppliers:
• 	Scale with your business
• 	Match your quality standards
• 	Help you grow your business
Product Sourcing
Method #1: Sourcing In-House
The FBI Method
Investigating your competitors’ suppliers
Step 1: Identify your competitor.
Step 2: Go on their website and check for their
company name.
Step 3: Plug in the company name on JungleScout.
Step 3: Choose a manufacturer under “Top
Suppliers.”
Method #2: External Sourcing Agent
Pros
• 	Available to visit many suppliers
Cons
• 	Limited supplier relationships
• 	May take a cut of the supplier’s fee (5-10%)
LESSON:
SOURCING QUALITY
SUPPLIERS

MANUFACTURING &
OPERATIONS AT SCALE
ACTION ITEMS
❏ 	Identify your direct competitors.

Cold Calling Scripts
Use the cold call script templates below to call your competitor’s suppliers.
The Business Card Approach:
Hi, my name is [Name] and I’m from an [Company, Description]. I met you guys at [Event/
Date] and someone gave me a business card of yours. I’d like to speak to someone who
looks after the [Your Country] market.
The Network Connection:
Hi, my name is [Name] and I’m from an [Company, Description]. I met you guys at [Event/
Date] and [Make Up a Name] recommended we reach out to you. I’d like to speak to
someone who looks after the [Your Country] market.

-- 12 of 25 --

In this lesson, you’ll learn how to negotiate with
suppliers to get the best possible quotes. Many
founders don’t know the extent to which you can
bargain for your product. Just implementing one of
the techniques I’m about to show you can save you
hundreds if not thousands of dollars.
The Double Spy Framework
❏ 	Approach two suppliers of a similar product.
❏ 	Get a quote from one you don’t intend to work
with.
❏ 	Drop it by 10%.
❏ 	Give the price to your dream supplier.
Pro Tips
1. 	Negotiate the down payment.
2. 	Meet your supplier in person.
3. 	Maintain a good relationship with your supplier.
4. 	Ask for a better payment term from the second
order on.
5. 	Include a quality policy in your contract.
6. 	Conduct quality control yourself of go with your
quality control team.
Standard Penalties
• 	Include 1% of hardware in the shipment in case
of damaged parts.
• 	Replenish the stock in the next shipment.
• 	Compensate 3x the cost.
LESSON:
NEGOTIATING WITH
SUPPLIERS
MODULE:
MANUFACTURING &
OPERATIONS AT SCALE
ACTION ITEMS

“Suppliers like to hear that you’re in it for the long haul.”

-- 15 of 25 --

In this lesson, I’ll teach you about all the ways in
which you could lose unprecedented amounts of
money in 3PL. It’s important to be aware of all of the
fees (often hidden fees) and factor them into your
P&L so you have accounted for as many as possible.
Warehousing
Pros
• 	Take stock anytime
• 	Ship anytime
• 	Potential for fast delivery time
• 	Control the consumer experience
Cons
• 	Pay lease
• 	Hire people
3PL
Pros
• 	No fixed costs
Cons
• 	Delivery dependent on external factors
• 	Fees
• 	Hidden fees
The Big Four 3PL Fees
• 	Inward freight
• 	Outward freight
• 	Storage
• 	Manual labor
LESSON:
PREVENTING 3PL BLEEDING
MODULE:
MANUFACTURING &
OPERATIONS AT SCALE

-- 17 of 25 --

In this lesson, I’ll teach you about the shipping terms
you need to know in order to avoid costly mistakes in
the process of getting your product to the consumer.
How to Avoid Unplanned Shipping Costs
Step 1: Understand COGS
• 	Cost of Goods Sold
• 	Direct costs attributable to the production of
goods (can include inward freight and duty, if
applicable)
Step 2: Nail down the trading terms
• 	Ex-Works (EXW): You (not the supplier are
responsible for the costs/liabilities for the entire
shipping process
• 	Free on Board (FOB): Supplier is responsible
until origin port and you’re responsible from
origin port to the customer
• 	Cost, Insurance and Freight (CIF or CNF):
Supplier is responsible for the costs/liabilities of
the entire shipping process
Step 3: Calculate your COGS
• 	Manufacturing Costs + Freight Forwarding +
Duty
Step 4: Track how price changes in each component
affect your P&L margins
LESSON:
INTERNATIONAL SHIPPING
MODULE:
MANUFACTURING &
OPERATIONS AT SCALE
ACTION ITEMS
❏ 	Download my trading terms workshop document in
the Resources.
❏ 	Make sure your quotes are in FOB.
COGs Per Shipping Method
• 	EXW
• Purchase cost
• Origin transportation/Customs clearance
• Destination transportation/customs clearance
• 	FOB
• Purchase cost
• International freight
• Destination transportation/customs clearance
• 	CIF or CNF
• Purchase cost
• Destination transportation/customs clearance
COURSE WORKBOOK
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

In this lesson, I’m going to give you the exact
questions you need to ask your Last Mile Delivery
provider to make sure you don’t get charged extra
fees.
Last Mile Delivery Costing Structure
[Consignment Charge + Weight Charge] x Fuel
Surcharge
LESSON:
LAST MILE DELIVERY (LMD)
MODULE:
MANUFACTURING &
OPERATIONS AT SCALE
ACTION ITEMS
❏ 	Re-evaluate the carriers your brand uses.
❏ 	Go through the questionnaire in the workbook.
Last Mile Delivery Questions
Ask your LMD provider the following questions prior
to initiating a partnership.
❏ 	What is your costing structure?
❏ 	Do you charge a re-delivery fee?
❏ 	Is ATL an option?
❏ 	Do you charge a storage fee?
❏ 	Do you charge a residential surcharge?
COURSE WORKBOOK

-- 21 of 25 --

In this lesson, I’ll teach you how to use Shippit and
similar apps to automate orders in your Shopify
store. Apps like these claim to reduce fulfillment
times by up to 80%. One caveat: apps like Shippit
are good if you’re processing up to 500 orders a day.
Anything more and you should look into a WMS or
Warehouse Management System.
App Features
• 	Order fulfillment and delivery automation
• 	Different carrier options depending on rates
• 	Live shipping status updates
LESSON:
AUTOMATING YOUR
DELIVERY (SHIPPIT)
MODULE:
MANUFACTURING &
OPERATIONS AT SCALE
